Traditional lending institutions have begun to pay close attention to this sector; institutions that can foresee trends in advance and complete their deployment through self-development or mergers and acquisitions will dominate the future landscape of the on-chain capital market. The profound impact of this financial revolution has long transcended the realm of fintech, exhibiting a two-way geopolitical effect. For individuals and businesses, access to the new global financial system means tangible economic empowerment: mitigating the risk of local currency devaluation, opening up global payment channels, and conducting business using the most liquid currency in the world—the US dollar. Farmers in sub-Saharan Africa, manufacturers in Southeast Asia, and small importers in Latin America can now independently hold, trade, and save US dollars without opening US bank accounts, relying on cross-border correspondent banking systems, or being subject to the access restrictions of traditional financial oligarchs. The US dollar is no longer a privileged resource but has become a universally beneficial tool—an unprecedented transformation. For the United States, stablecoins further strengthen the global dominance of the US dollar. Since the last century, the dollar's hegemony has been maintained through the International Monetary Fund, the World Bank, a global correspondent banking network, and bilateral agreements, allowing the US Treasury and the Federal Reserve to control global financial rules for a long time. Now, stablecoins have opened up a more direct new path: every wallet holding a dollar-denominated stablecoin is a new node in the dollar financial network, capable of completing value settlements between any two locations globally at near-zero cost and in seconds. The higher the adoption rate of stablecoins, the stronger the network effect, allowing the dollar to further penetrate previously limited economies. This is the most far-reaching geopolitical consequence of the stablecoin era: with the implementation of regulations such as the GENIUS Act, the US government is not simply regulating a new type of financial product, but rather actively making a strategic bet—relying on stablecoin infrastructure to consolidate the dollar's long-term dominant position. This strategy is of great significance at a time when the dollar's hegemony faces unprecedented challenges after the collapse of the Bretton Woods system. Beyond Payments: A Fundamental Restructuring of Global Finance The new technology stack for global finance is still under construction, and its strategic value extends far beyond payment upgrades. What is currently happening is a comprehensive, fundamental upgrade of the financial system. The new underlying transmission network is inherently open, programmable, and interconnected, capable of covering regions, populations, and business scenarios never before touched by traditional finance. Its value extends beyond low-cost global payments; it also includes: providing inclusive dollar services to regions where local banks are ineffective; generating added value for massive amounts of idle funds; providing credit support to groups lacking traditional financial services; and opening investment doors for billions of people who have never participated in capital markets. Today, companies deeply involved in the entire financial infrastructure chain and strategically positioned at each level will define the next era of global financial landscape and dominate the future direction of the global dollar economy.

data.title: Solana releases quantum roadmap; Trump discusses new Iran proposal with his team.
data.description:
Headline

▌Solana Releases Quantum Roadmap: Falcon Scheme Selected, Existing Migration Work Ready

On April 27th, Solana officially published an article on the potential impact of quantum computing on blockchain, reiterating that the quantum threat is still several years away, but the Solana ecosystem has made ample research and technical preparations in advance. The independent verification client development teams Anza and Firedancer (collectively representing the majority of the network's staking share) unanimously agreed after independent research that a post-quantum digital signature scheme with compact signatures suitable for high-throughput blockchains was needed, and jointly selected the Falcon scheme. Both have released initial implementation code on GitHub. In addition, the Solana Winternitz Vault developed by Blueshift has been running in the ecosystem for over two years, providing a direct quantum-resistant path. Earlier this year, Google Quantum AI listed this Vault as a leading case of forward-looking post-quantum work in its white paper.

Solana's current quantum roadmap includes: continuing quantum research and evaluating Falcon and alternatives; adopting a post-quantum solution for new wallets when quantum becomes a credible threat; and migrating existing wallets to the selected solution. Officials state that the migration is under control and can be completed quickly, with no significant impact on network performance expected.

▌White House: Trump and his national security team met to discuss new Iranian proposal

On April 28, White House Press Secretary Levitt confirmed at a press conference on the 27th that President Trump and his national security team met that day to discuss a new negotiating proposal from Iran. Levitt said she was unsure if the meeting had ended, but the proposal was indeed under discussion. However, she "didn't want to speak before the president or his national security team." According to a report by the US news website Axios on the 26th, a US official and two informed sources stated that the US had received a new negotiating proposal from Iran, delivered through Pakistan. The new Iranian negotiating proposal primarily focuses on resolving the Strait of Hormuz crisis and the US naval blockade against Iran.

... As part of the plan, the ceasefire would be extended indefinitely, or the parties would agree to a permanent end to the conflict. Nuclear negotiations would only begin after the Strait of Hormuz reopens and the United States lifts its blockade. (Xinhua News Agency) Market Data As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $77,222.12, a 24-hour change of -1.7%; ETH price is $2,295.85, a 24-hour change of -3.2%; BNB price is $625.93, a 24-hour change of -1.5%. SOL price is $84.69, a 24-hour change of -2.6%; DOGE price is $0.09895, a 24-hour change of -0.3%; XRP price is $1.40, a 24-hour change of -2.2%; TRX price is $0.3252, a 24-hour change of +0.5%. The WLFI price is $0.07244, a 24-hour change of -3.5%; the HYPE price is $41.58, a 24-hour change of -1.9%. Policy: The CFTC sues New York State for control over prediction markets. On April 27, according to the Wall Street Journal, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) filed a lawsuit against New York State in the Southern District of New York, seeking a court ruling that the CFTC has exclusive regulatory authority over prediction markets to prevent New York State's crackdown. Previously, New York State had filed lawsuits against cryptocurrency exchanges Coinbase and Gemini regarding their prediction market businesses. Earlier this month, the CFTC also filed similar lawsuits against Arizona, Illinois, and Connecticut over similar issues, escalating the jurisdictional dispute between federal and state regulators. On April 28, White House Press Secretary Levitt revealed to reporters that President Trump discussed a new proposal from Iran aimed at resolving the war with his senior national security advisors. According to the proposal, the Strait of Hormuz would remain open, while the issue of Iran's nuclear program would be discussed at a later date. When answering questions at the press conference, Levitt did not comment specifically on the proposal. However, she stated that Trump's bottom line remains unchanged. Trump demands that the Strait of Hormuz, an oil shipping route, remain open and that Iran hand over its enriched uranium. Levitt said, "I wouldn't say they're 'considering' the proposal. I can only say that there was indeed a discussion this morning, and I don't want to preemptively speak before the president makes a formal statement; I'm sure you'll hear directly from the president on this topic later." ▌US Media: Trump Skeptical of Iran's Proposal but Not Outright Rejection On April 28, according to the Wall Street Journal, US officials said that US President Trump and his national security team are skeptical of a recent proposal for a deal with Iran. Under this proposal, Iran would ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open and suspend discussions about its nuclear program. Trump discussed the proposal with aides Monday morning local time. Officials said that while Trump did not outright reject it, he expressed doubts about whether Iran would act in good faith and whether it would be willing to meet his key demands. Trump's key demands are: Iran to halt its nuclear enrichment activities and pledge never to develop nuclear weapons. These officials said the US will continue negotiations with Iran, and the White House may respond and offer counter-proposals in the coming days.

▌Iranian Official Says Nuclear Issue Not Negotiable

On April 27, Ibrahim Aziz, Chairman of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, stated in an interview broadcast on April 26 that Iran has no intention of negotiating with the United States, especially on the nuclear issue—because Iran firmly believes in its right to enrich uranium, and its uranium enrichment activities must be considered a fait accompli. He explained that even if Iran were to hand over more than 400 kilograms of enriched uranium as demanded by the United States, the problem would not be resolved, and the United States would only intensify its efforts. He emphasized that the reason for Iran's victory was "precisely our resistance, our firm stance, and our thwarting of the United States' attempts to push its luck."

... (Dongxin News Agency) US Secretary of State Rubio: Iran “Very Serious” About Agreement On April 28, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that Iran is “seriously” interested in reaching an agreement with the United States, but any agreement must prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons. “I think they genuinely want to get out of their current predicament,” Rubio said. He pointed to Iran’s deteriorating economic situation, including inflation, difficulty in paying wages, and ongoing sanctions, stating, “All the problems Iran had before this conflict started remain, and most of them have gotten worse.” “We must ensure that any agreement reached…completely prevents them from accelerating their development of nuclear weapons at any time,” he added. (Al Jazeera) Blockchain Applications Tether Launches Mining Development Kit (MDK) Open Infrastructure Layer for Bitcoin Mining On April 27th, Tether announced the launch of the Mining Development Kit (MDK). MDK is an open-source, full-stack development framework designed to provide Bitcoin miners and developers with unified control over their entire infrastructure stack. MDK employs an open, modular architecture and Agnostic interfaces, combining a JavaScript backend SDK and a React UI component library to replace the closed, proprietary systems that have fragmented mining operations for years. The framework is suitable for all users, from home miners to gigawatt-scale mining farms, supports Windows, macOS, and Linux, and avoids vendor lock-in. MDK includes a standalone core layer (MDK Core) and a UI development kit, enabling rapid development of operational dashboards, automated workflows, mining pool management tools, and analytics pipelines. Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino stated that MDK will provide the infrastructure support for the next generation of Bitcoin mining focused on automation and optimization. Tether had previously open-sourced its mining operating system, MOS.

▌Gemini Launches "Agentic Trading" Feature, Allowing AI Bots to Directly Manage Crypto Trading Accounts

On April 27th, the cryptocurrency trading platform Gemini launched the Agentic Trading feature, allowing users to connect AI models such as Claude and ChatGPT to their trading accounts. Gemini claims this is "the first agentic trading tool directly offered through a US-regulated trading platform." This feature operates through the MCP open standard developed by Anthropic, and Gemini has integrated its complete trading API with MCP. The AI ​​can autonomously monitor the market, place orders, and execute trades and manage risk based on predefined strategies.

... Gemini stated, "Proxy trading is not just a feature, but a new paradigm: AI handles execution, pattern recognition, and discipline, while users focus on strategy and goals." Furthermore, the feature includes a series of modular functions called "trading skills," such as querying bid-ask spreads and retrieving historical data for backtesting, with more features to be introduced in the future. Cryptocurrency A16z crypto released a report titled "Global Financial New Stack: Stablecoin Edition." The report points out that stablecoins have evolved from niche trading tools into fundamental financial pipelines, giving rise to a new "banking as a service" model and driving the reconstruction of the financial system. The report argues that the transition to on-chain finance has "crossed the point of no return." The report categorizes blockchains into three types: general-purpose chains (such as Solana, Ethereum, and L2), payment-specific chains (such as Stripe's Tempo), and institutional networks (such as Canton). It also points out that banking bottlenecks are easing, with a number of crypto-friendly banks actively connecting on-chain infrastructure with traditional fiat currency systems. The competition for stablecoin issuance has shifted towards regulatory positioning, with issuers vying for OCC (Original Code of Conduct) national trust licenses. The report states that payments are the "first act," while credit may be the more important "second act." Large-scale issuance of stablecoins will spur new on-chain credit markets, enabling capital to be formed outside the traditional banking system. The report further emphasizes that stablecoins not only strengthen the dominance of the US dollar but also provide emerging market users with access to the US dollar.

▌Report: Bitcoin Leads Crypto Market Stabilization, On-Chain Data Shows Positive Signals

On April 27th, Fidelity Digital Assets released its "2026 Q2 Signals Report," pointing out that although the crypto market was generally consolidating at the beginning of the second quarter, several underlying data points showed signs of stabilization. The report shows that Bitcoin's dominance continues to rise, capital continues to concentrate on the most liquid assets, and unrealized profitability and momentum indicators are consistent with the characteristics of a correction period, potentially laying the foundation for a more stable market structure in the future. Meanwhile, Ethereum and Solana's network usage and price movements have diverged, indicating that demand at the protocol layer remains robust. The report also points out that Bitcoin futures are currently experiencing negative funding rates, which research firm 10x believes reflects structural hedging behavior by institutions rather than an overall bearish signal.

▌Data: BlackRock's IBIT Sees Net Inflows of $983 Million in a Single Week, a Six-Month High

On April 27th, BlackRock's IBIT spot Bitcoin ETF saw net inflows of $983 million in a single week, a six-month high.

▌Strategy Added 3,273 Bitcoins Last Week

On April 27th, according to official news, Strategy added 3,273 BTC last week at an average price of approximately $77,906, for a total value of approximately $255 million. Its BTC return since the beginning of 2026 has reached 9.6%.

As of April 26, 2026, Strategy held a total of 818,334 BTC, with a total purchase cost of approximately $61.81 billion and an average price of approximately $755.37 million. Strive increased its holdings by 789 BTC, bringing its total holdings to 14,557 BTC. On April 27, Strive CEO Matt Cole stated on social media that Strive spent approximately $61.43 million to acquire 789 BTC, with an average price of approximately $77,890. As of April 24, Strive held a total of 14,557 BTC. BitMine increased its holdings by 101,901 ETH last week, bringing its total holdings to approximately 5.078 million ETH. According to a PR Newswire report on April 27, BitMine increased its holdings by 101,901 Ethereum last week, the highest weekly purchase since the week of December 15, 2025. As of April 26, 2026, its total Ethereum holdings reached 5,078,386 ETH, representing approximately 4.21% of the total Ethereum supply. Currently, BitMine holds approximately $13.3 billion in cryptocurrency, cash, and other investment assets, including $940 million in cash, 200 Bitcoins, $200 million in equity in Beast Industries, and a $91 million investment in Eightco Holdings (ORBS). Additionally, it has staked 3,701,589 Ethereum (73% of its total holdings), valued at approximately $8.8 billion, with a current annualized staking yield of approximately $264 million. Circle Ventures Announces Purchase of AAVE Tokens to Support the DeFi Community On April 27th, Circle posted on social media that Circle Ventures is purchasing AAVE tokens because robust DeFi infrastructure doesn't build itself. Aave is helping to shape the future of on-chain finance, and Circle is supporting this ecosystem and the entire community built around it.

▌Aave Labs Proposes Arbitrum to Unfreeze $73.5 Million in ETH Related to the KelpDAO Attack

On April 27, Aave Labs proposed Arbitrum to unfreeze $73.5 million in ETH related to the KelpDAO attack and reallocate it to the "DeFi United" relief fund to compensate affected rsETH holders.

▌Lido: 2,500 ETH Aave Aid Proposal Has Reached Statistical Citation, Expected to be Implemented Next Week

On April 27, Lido updated the progress of the Kelp security incident on the X platform, disclosing that the 2,500 ETH aid proposal contributed by Lido has passed the main voting stage and reached the statistical quorum. Voting will end at 20:09 UTC on April 28, and final implementation is expected to be completed next week.

Meanwhile, Lido Earn, Mellow Protocol, and the Ethereum Foundation have reached a bilateral swap agreement for approximately 21,269 aWETH and wstETH, helping the EarnETH vault directly repay WETH debt on Aave, reducing borrowing costs and improving vault health. Furthermore, Lido emphasized that stETH and wstETH were unaffected by last week's events, and the Lido staking protocol is operating normally.

▌Spark Releases Q1 2026 Financial Report: Net Protocol Surplus of $3.46 Million

On April 27th, the Spark protocol released its financial report for the first quarter of 2026. The report shows that the gross protocol return for the quarter was $31.5 million (a 31% decrease quarter-over-quarter), net protocol return was $6.91 million (a 30% decrease quarter-over-quarter), and net protocol surplus was $3.46 million (a 47% decrease quarter-over-quarter). The protocol vault size at the end of the quarter reached $46.1 million (a 5.7% increase quarter-over-quarter).

... In addition, Spark launched an SPK token buyback program, investing $986,000 to repurchase tokens through the open market. The revenue structure shifted this quarter, with distribution rewards becoming the protocol's largest source of net returns ($3.31 million), surpassing the net revenue of the Spark Liquidity Layer (SLL) for the first time. SLL's average deployed capital was $1.93 billion, with an average annualized yield of 5.8%. SparkLend continued to support institutional lending, and its USDT savings treasury continued to grow. Spark's institutional lending products deployed $150 million at the end of the quarter, and governance has approved its $1 billion cap. The report notes that unfavorable DeFi lending market conditions led to a narrowing of SLL spreads, but the protocol's distribution business grew significantly. USDS, as a scalable savings-based reward mechanism, continues to expand its distribution channels to multiple chains and various stablecoins in this challenging market environment.

▌Consensys Joins DeFi United, Pledges Up to 30,000 ETH to Support rsETH Recovery

On April 27th, Consensys and ethereumJoseph officially joined DeFi United, providing up to 30,000 ETH in financial support for the recovery of rsETH. Sharplink will also continue to provide strategic advisory support. AAVE stated that their contribution is a crucial part of DeFi United's overall effort to restore rsETH collateral and stabilize market conditions; without them, the recovery efforts would not have achieved their current progress.

... **Important Economic Developments** Morgan Stanley believes Warsh's nomination to the Federal Reserve as Chairman is likely to be confirmed, which will bring long-term institutional risks and could exacerbate volatility in the US Treasury market. A team led by Matthew Hornbach pointed out that under Warsh's leadership, the Fed may adopt new inflation indicators, reduce forward guidance, and push for a reduction in the size of its balance sheet. These changes "could increase volatility between each policy meeting." Warsh himself predicted a "mechanistic change in the way the central bank operates" during his testimony to Congress last week.

▌Barclays: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady This Week, But Rate Cuts Still Possible This Year

On April 27, Barclays analysts stated in a report that, given the persistently high inflation, the Federal Reserve is expected to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at its meeting this week, but a rate cut is still possible this year. The analysts said, "In a highly uncertain environment, the Fed is inclined to hold rates steady, with strong demand and still relatively high inflation supporting its patience, and policymakers have also signaled a weakening of confidence in further rate cuts in the near term." The analysts stated that if inflation falls as expected, the Fed is expected to gain sufficient confidence to begin easing policy around September. "We still expect it to cut rates this year." According to LSEG data, the money market currently prices in a 10 basis point rate cut by the Fed in 2026.

... (Jinshi) On April 27th, independent investment research firm BCA Research released a research report indicating that the artificial intelligence (AI) investment boom is gradually entering the final stage of its cycle, and a rapid surge similar to the dot-com bubble era may be imminent. BCA had already warned of the risks of this AI melt-up in its 2026 annual outlook report, believing that this surge could drive the US stock market up by as much as 30%, potentially pushing the S&P 500 index above the 9200 mark. A "melt-up" refers to a rapid rise in the stock market driven by sentiment and momentum, even when fundamentals may not be supportive; it is often the last wave of frenzy before a crash. (Dongxin News Agency)

▌Economist: Warsh's Stance May Be Slightly Dovish Than Powell

On April 28, as the obstacles to Kevin Warsh being confirmed as the next Federal Reserve Chairman appear to be decreasing, the market is reassessing what this change might bring. AMP Chief Economist Shane Oliver said that Warsh is committed to maintaining the Fed's independence, and he may prioritize AI transformation over employment. Oliver said he may also prioritize cut-off mean inflation over core PCE, although this could be seen as a selective approach. Oliver added that his stance may be slightly more dovish than Powell's, but there won't be a fundamental difference.

...

▌Nvidia's stock price hits a new high after 6 months

On April 28, Nvidia's stock price rose more than 2%, hitting an all-time high of $212.6 during trading, a new high after 6 months, with a market capitalization of $5.17 trillion during the session.

▌The probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged this week is 100%

On April 28, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 100%. The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by June is 4.5%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 95.5%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" During Geopolitical Crises?

Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been supported by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation).

Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," which can indeed function when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries are reduced, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but rather a completely new "next-generation gold."

Generational shifts and the widespread application of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: Lowering expectations for the next BTC bull market
data.description:
Author: Alex Xu Source: X, @xuxiaopengmint

BTC has been my largest holding in total assets for most of the past few years (it is no longer).

During this BTC bull market cycle, I unwound the small leverage I had added during the deep bear market (around 1.1-1.2 times, done through BTC collateralized lending) at 70,000, and reduced my BTC position from fully invested to around 30% at 100,000-120,000.

There were also some minor operations during this period, such as adding a small amount to my position when BTC corrected to 50,000+ in 2024, and adding a small amount to my position when BTC spiked to 60,000 in February this year. These operations were all based on my long-term bullish view on BTC.

Based on past cyclical logic, now is a good time to accumulate more BTC and wait for the next bull market cycle. However, during the recent BTC rebound, I further reduced my already low 30% BTC position at the 78,000-79,000 level. It's essential to continuously monitor one's assets and conduct regular fundamental reviews. Reducing my BTC position is also an action taken after continuous review and deliberation. The conclusion is that I will lower my expectation for the peak value of BTC in the next bull market. Let's analyze the reasons: First, the potential energy driving the next BTC cycle to continue its surge is not as strong as in previous cycles. Previous BTC cycles actually anticipated an exponential expansion of the investor base, from niche geek financial experiments to mainstream and even institutional asset allocation; this narrative has gradually materialized in each cycle. For example, in the 2023-2025 cycle, it entered the holdings of mainstream financial institutions through the listing of compliant ETF products, and received strong support from Trafi financial institutions represented by BlackRock, plus strong endorsement from the president of the world's largest economy. If we want to elevate the narrative to another level, its next round of inclusion needs to be on the balance sheets of leading sovereign nations, such as: 1. More sovereign wealth (currently mainly Abu Dhabi) 2. Central bank reserves * Pure government fiscal reserves (such as those of US states) may not be sufficient, as their purchasing power is relatively small, far less than that of traditional financial institutions. However, in my view, achieving this leap in the next 2-3 years will be quite difficult. This bull market initially saw expectations that Bitcoin would be included in the US Federal Reserve, but this hope was largely disproven last year. Currently, very few US states have even passed Bitcoin reserve laws. At its peak in early 2025, over 20 US states were pushing for Bitcoin reserve bills; now, only a small number have passed, and some are even incomplete, requiring separate proposals and approvals for their purchase budgets. Currently, major central banks haven't shown significant interest in BTC. Its short history of consensus, high volatility, and the presence of gold as a competitor make it very difficult for BTC to be included on central bank balance sheets. Secondly, there's the increased opportunity cost for me personally. Over the past six months, I've gradually discovered many good companies with attractive prices, which will be the main focus of my portfolio adjustments (another part is increasing cash reserves). Thirdly, there's the negative impact of the overall crypto industry downturn on Bitcoin demand and consensus. Currently, very few business models in the crypto industry are truly viable. Most Web3 models (socialfi, gamefi, depin, distributed storage/computing power, etc.) have been gradually proven false over time. In reality, only DeFi has truly generated positive cash flow and profits. However, DeFi's overall development in the latter half of this cycle has been relatively mediocre, primarily due to the shrinking of high-quality native assets in the industry, leading to a contraction in DeFi businesses (still mainly lending and DEX trading). The shrinking of the entire crypto industry's core base, along with the reduction in practitioners and investors, will also slow the growth of BTC holdings and even cause some to be lost. *Hypeliquid, as an on-chain exchange, is an outlier that has grown against the trend. However, its success largely stems from its takeover of the existing market share on CEXs and its later-stage inclusion of 24/7 trading in asset classes outside of crypto (commodities, US stocks, pre-IPO assets), resulting in relatively little value transfer to BTC. Hypeliquid, relying on regulatory arbitrage and operating as a lone player, is unlikely to hedge against the industry-wide trend of contraction (the impact of prediction markets is similar). Fourth, the financing costs for Strategy, BTC's largest buyer, continue to rise. Its primary financing method is currently through the issuance of perpetual preferred stock (STRC), with financing rates already reaching 11.5%, and it will soon be changing from monthly interest payments to bi-weekly payments, otherwise it will be unable to stabilize the market price of STRC. This gives me a rather uneasy feeling, although Strategy's current financial situation is still far from a potential collapse. Furthermore, we can see that, apart from Strategy, almost all the previously very active BTC DAT concept stocks have died out, leaving only Strategy standing alone. Strategy doesn't need an actual collapse to suppress BTC prices; as the largest listed holder and net buyer of BTC, a decrease in its buying speed and depletion of its financing capacity will trigger significant marginal selling pressure. Fifth, gold, Bitcoin's main competitor in the non-sovereign asset sector (value proposition: hedging against fiat currency inflation), has narrowed the gap with Bitcoin in terms of product offerings. We previously stated that "digital gold" Bitcoin is superior to gold because it has better divisibility, portability, verifiability, and decentralization. However, this round has seen the emergence of "tokenized gold," a product that is no different from Bitcoin in terms of verifiability, portability, and divisibility, and its scale is growing rapidly. [Image of tokenized commodity assets from rwa.xyz, showing tokenized gold as the majority.] Of course, many people will say that tokenized gold relies on centralized credit, but in my opinion, reliance on centralized credit is not a necessary condition in the crypto industry, because one of the core infrastructures of the entire crypto industry—stablecoins—is mostly based on completely centralized credit. Sixth, with the halving, Bitcoin's security budget shortfall is becoming increasingly severe (explorations of new fee sources such as inscription and BTC L2 have largely failed). This is a recurring topic, but it remains a problem. Conversely, I don't think quantum computing poses a significant threat; the community already has solutions. Of course, even after reducing my holdings, I'm still bullish on BTC; otherwise, I should have sold everything. It remains one of my largest holdings, and I hope it will continue to rise. Other questions might arise: Why reduce now? Because of the recent significant rebound, so I'm reducing my holdings. What if the price rises after I reduce my holdings? If the reasons for my pessimism become less valid due to changes in the external and internal environment, or if more previously unforeseen positive factors emerge, and the price wasn't too high at the time, I will buy it back. If the price was too high to justify buying back then, it means my understanding of the asset wasn't commensurate with it, and I will accept the outcome. This is just my personal opinion and for reference only.
